channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: President Samia Swears in Newly Appointed Ministers in Dodoma
item.description: On April 8, 2026, President Samia Suluhu Hassan officially swore in several newly appointed ministers during a ceremony at Chamwino State House in Dodoma. Among those sworn in was Palamagamba Kabudi, ...

item.title: Capitals defeat Maple Leafs 4-0 as Thompson earns third shutout of the season
item.description: On Wednesday night in Toronto, the Washington Capitals revived their playoff hopes with a decisive 4-0 win against the Toronto Maple Leafs. Goaltender Logan Thompson was instrumental, stopping all 21 ...

item.title: BTS launches 'ARIRANG' comeback world tour covering 34 cities worldwide
item.description: BTS, the global K-pop sensation, officially marks their return to the world stage with the launch of their 'ARIRANG' world tour this April 9, 2026. Following a three-year break for mandatory military ...

item.title: SA faces challenges in producing a unified female boxing world champion, says Malinga
item.description: Veteran South African boxing promoter Zandile Malinga has expressed scepticism about the country producing a unified female boxing world champion in the near future. Despite having four women champion...

item.title: India and Bangladesh agree to deepen bilateral ties under new administration
item.description: External Affairs Minister Dr S Jaishankar met Bangladeshi Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman in New Delhi on Wednesday to discuss ways to strengthen bilateral relations between India and Bangladesh. Bot...

item.title: Northern Ontario driver charged with multiple offences following traffic stop
item.description: A man in Northern Ontario has been charged with several offences following an early-morning traffic stop. The incident reportedly involved a vehicle operated without a seatbelt and with no functional ...

item.title: Indian Stock Market Overview Amid US-Iran Ceasefire and Global Oil Price Movements

item.title: Multiple Attacks by Bandits in Sokoto Kill 5 and Kidnap 22 People
item.description: For Sabon Birni Local Government Area of Sokoto State, na serious wahala happen as bandits attack three communities for midnight between Tuesday night and Wednesday morning. For Sabon Birni town, dem ...

item.title: APC Claims Opposition Parties Lack Readiness for National Leadership
item.description: The National Chairman of the All Progressives Congress (APC), Professor Nentawe Yilwatda, don talk say di wahala wey dey inside opposition parties show say dem never ready to take charge of Nigeria. F...

item.title: Zimbabwe's Democracy: Rethinking Accountability Beyond Elections
item.description: In Southern Africa, democracy is often viewed through the lens of elections, where citizens pass judgment on their leaders. However, this perspective overlooks the crucial question: What happens after...

item.title: Tanzania’s President Samia Takes Action to Cut Fuel Use Amid Global Oil Crisis
item.description: In response to the global surge in oil prices, President Samia Suluhu Hassan of Tanzania has directed government officials to reduce fuel consumption by cutting down on unnecessary travel and limiting...

item.title: Doctor Convicted of Attempted Manslaughter After Attacking Wife on Hawaii Cliff
item.description: Gerhardt Konig, a 47-year-old anesthesiologist, was found guilty of attempted manslaughter after attacking his wife, Arielle, on a cliffside hike in Hawaii. The incident, which took place on March 24,...

item.title: Belgian Photographer Creates Extensive Catalog of Personal Possessions
item.description: Belgian photographer Barbara Iweins has undertaken a remarkable and meticulous project: documenting every single item she owns through photography and detailed cataloging. The resulting collection enc...

item.title: India: Polling Underway in Several States Amidst Encouragement for High Voter Participation
item.description: Polling commenced today across several Indian states, including Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry, marking a significant phase in the ongoing electoral process. Prime Minister Narendra Modi has actively e...

item.title: Ceasefire under strain as Iran closes Strait of Hormuz and Israel intensifies strikes on Lebanon
item.description: The fragile ceasefire between the United States, Israel and Iran is under serious pressure after Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed again, while Israel launched one of its heaviest bombardments...

item.title: Woman Publicly Apologises to Boyfriend Using Hired Men with Placards in Nairobi
item.description: In Nairobi, a woman known as Diana caused a stir after she hired several men to publicly apologise to her banker boyfriend, Brian, following a disagreement in their relationship. The apology, which pl...

item.title: Experts call for urgent action on mpox outbreak in Khairpur affecting newborns
item.description: Health experts at the Aga Khan University Hospital in Karachi have raised serious concerns over the mpox outbreak reported in Khairpur district of Sindh. Last week, the Sindh health department confirm...

item.title: Lori Loughlin Embraces New Chapter, Dating Again After Divorce from Mossimo Giannulli
item.description: After her split from Mossimo Giannulli in October 2025, Lori Loughlin is reportedly stepping back into the dating scene. Sources close to the actress revealed that she is enjoying life and taking a li...

item.title: Kanye West Performs on Stage with Daughter North in Los Angeles
item.description: On April 1, 2026, Kanye West invited his 12-year-old daughter, North West, to join him on stage during his concert at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles. The father-daughter duo performed their songs “Talkin...

item.title: Brexit's Long-lasting Divide: A Decade On, Britain Remains Polarised
item.description: A decade after the Brexit referendum, the UK remains deeply divided, with tribal politics continuing to shape the nation's identity. The referendum on 23 June 2016 fundamentally altered the way voters...
